title: MD
biography: Andreas Gruntzig was a German-born cardiologist who performed the first successful coronary balloon angioplasty in 1977 in Zurich. His invention of percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty (PTCA) transformed cardiology by offering a non-surgical alternative to bypass surgery for blocked arteries.
aiSummary: Andreas Gruntzig was a cardiology specialist who invented coronary balloon angioplasty in 1977. His technique revolutionized treatment of coronary artery disease. Based in Zurich, Switzerland.

timeline.title: First peripheral balloon angioplasty
timeline.description: Performed first balloon dilatation of a peripheral artery

timeline.title: First coronary balloon angioplasty
timeline.description: Performed the first PTCA on a conscious patient with coronary artery disease in Zurich

timeline.title: Moved to Emory University
timeline.description: Brought angioplasty technique to the United States, training cardiologists worldwide
